chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly provides a working link to the GitHub repository (https://github.com/imperial-qore/CAROL) in the 'SOFTWARE AVAILABILITY' section, which contains the code and result reproduction scripts.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation is conducted on a physical testbed consisting of 16 Raspberry Pi 4B nodes with heterogeneous RAM configurations, running Ubuntu-18.04 LTS and Docker containers, rather than purely theoretical or standard software simulations.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: Section III-A contains a dedicated 'Fault Model' subsection that explicitly defines the Byzantine failure model, specifying recoverable faults (CPU overload, RAM contention, Disk attack, DDOS) caused by resource over-utilization, while ignoring unrecoverable outages.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides rigorous data-driven analysis of trade-offs in Section V-C and Figure 5(f), quantifying specific overheads such as decision time (6.77% higher than heuristic baseline), memory consumption (~5%), and fine-tuning overhead (35.62% lower than baselines).

major_weakness: The paper does not exhibit any major weaknesses as it achieved the maximum score (2) across all four evaluated methodological criteria.
